new_classes.definition_en: Blended Scotch Whisky produced by blending one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies, classified under CN subheadings 2208 30 71 (containers of 2 litres or less) and 2208 30 79 (containers of more than 2 litres).

new_classes.label_en: Blended Scotch Whisky in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22083071)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Scotch Whisky in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 Litern oder weniger (KN 22083071)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended Scotch Whisky produced by blending one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less, classified under CN subheading 2208 30 71.

Process of blending one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies to produce Blended Scotch Whisky, as required by the legal definition applicable to CN subheadings 2208 30 71 and 2208 30 79.

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria from the legal text are: (1) the product type is 'blended Scotch whisky', (2) it is produced by blending one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies, (3) the geographic origin is Scotland, and (4) for CN 22083071, the container size is 2 litres or less. All four criteria are captured: the BlendedScotchWhisky class encodes criteria 1–3 (via hasGeographicOriginQuality pointing to ScotlandDistillationOrigin and hasBlendingProcess pointing to ScotchWhiskyBlendingProcess), and the BlendedScotchWhiskySmallContainer subclass encodes criterion 4 (via maxContainerVolumeL hasValue 2.0 and codeString hasValue '22083071'). A minor gap remains: the exact minimum number of component whiskies (≥1 single malt AND ≥1 single grain) is expressed semantically only through the blending process class description rather than as a formal OWL cardinality constraint, since individual whisky components are not modelled at instance level. Coverage is therefore 0.85.
